trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Sweden exports machinery, vehicles, pharmaceuticals, and telecom equipment to Thailand; Thailand exports electronics, vehicles, rubber, and food products to Sweden. IKEA and other Swedish firms have Thailand manufacturing operations.

diplomatic.summary: Sweden and Thailand maintain diplomatic relations established in 1868, making it one of Sweden's longest-standing bilateral relationships in Asia. People-to-people ties are strong owing to significant Thai diaspora in Sweden. Sweden has periodically raised human rights concerns regarding Thailand's post-2014 political situation.

military.summary: No military alliance or significant defence cooperation. Sweden focuses its defence engagement on NATO and European partners. Thailand's military cooperation is centred on the US and ASEAN neighbours.

financial.summary: Swedish firms (Volvo, Ericsson, AstraZeneca, IKEA) invest in Thailand through manufacturing and distribution. Swedish pension and sovereign funds hold Thai equities as part of emerging market exposure. Thai FDI in Sweden is negligible.

energy.summary: Minimal direct bilateral energy trade. Sweden's energy mix is dominated by renewables and nuclear; Thailand imports oil and gas from the Gulf and regional sources. No direct energy supply relationship.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation through Sida (Swedish development aid) funded research partnerships and bilateral academic exchanges. Collaboration in tropical medicine, sustainable development, and environmental sciences.
